commits:
[{'sha': 'a5181d2fd6d149dac10a7f888be0c46ba4a34d89', 'message': "Remove `rfail` support from incremental tests

Incremental revisions beginning with `rfail` would cause the incremental test
runner to build the test program, expecting success, and then run the test
program, expecting failure.

Expecting incremental tests to fail at runtime is of questionable utility,
because in almost all cases an equivalent test program can be made to succeed
at runtime instead.

Removing `rfail` support is a small step towards cleaning up compiletest's
incremental test runner, and its overall handling of pass/fail expectations.

There was one existing regression test using `rfail` revisions:
`tests/incremental/issue-80691-bad-eval-cache.rs`. The test code is complex,
and reverting the fix in RUST-83220 does not actually cause the test to fail,
suggesting that it is no longer a useful regression test. This commit therefore
deletes that test."}, {'sha': '56603e486769118a89d55a2a76a251e34d4ee9a1', 'message': 'rename `{ref=>imm_ptr}_to_mplace`, `mplace_to_{ref=>imm_ptr}`'}, {'sha': '407259b2ff7ebe66eaacd566570a7ac7d28b3e19', 'message': 'allow `mplace_to_imm_ptr` to return different types of pointers'}, {'sha': 'd5f98fbb27f6f31a7d211353b016941fa45f699d', 'message': 'implement `StructuralPartialEq` for `MaybeDangling`

This fixes a stable-to-stable regression where constants of type
`ManuallyDrop<T>` would not be allowed to be used as a pattern due to
`MaybeDangling<T>` in `ManuallyDrop<T>` not implementing
`StructuralPartialEq`.'}, {'sha': '31c694d71b7d3c6fa5f0b66eab3092dc5fda370f', 'message': 'Update books'}, {'sha': 'fc71bad873a361c98a6d1db74bc72ccb6503a098', 'message': 'de-non_const some iterator methods'}, {'sha': '3ff4201fd132f5b46d504d49c4fd116772b694a1', 'message': "Move `rustc_middle::mir::mono` to `rustc_middle::mono`

Because the things in this module aren't MIR and don't use anything
from `rustc_middle::mir`. Also, modules that use `mono` often don't use
anything else from `rustc_middle::mir`."}, {'sha': 'b19364c4aa52792896436d39a04831d9c8aec60a', 'message': "Rollup merge of #154627 - nnethercote:mv-rustc_middle-mir-mono, r=saethlin

Move `rustc_middle::mir::mono` to `rustc_middle::mono`

Because the things in this module aren't MIR and don't use anything from `rustc_middle::mir`. Also, modules that use `mono` often don't use anything else from `rustc_middle::mir`.

r? @saethlin"}, {'sha': '312913e802810fa07e8ad7b9ff43bec8e71e2f29', 'message': 'Rollup merge of #154729 - Lars-Schumann:de-non_const-iterator, r=oli-obk

de-non_const some `Iterator` methods

https://github.com/rust-lang/rust/issues/92476

r? oli-obk

this is essentially just https://github.com/rust-lang/rust/pull/153708 again (which I accidentally closed...), but with the changes to `advance_by` omitted, since that seems to have caused the ICE.'}, {'sha': '7913288d6600bd762301a34177fc24f45532105d', 'message': "Rollup merge of #154891 - WaffleLapkin:deregress-manually-drop-matching, r=JohnTitor

implement `StructuralPartialEq` for `MaybeDangling`

This fixes -- a stable-to-stable regression where constants of type `ManuallyDrop<T>` would not be allowed to be used as a pattern due to `MaybeDangling<T>` in `ManuallyDrop<T>` not implementing `StructuralPartialEq`.

Fixes https://github.com/rust-lang/rust/issues/154890

I'm sorry, @theemathas, I forgot to address your [comment](https://github.com/rust-lang/rust/pull/149614#discussion_r2587340079) :sob:"}, {'sha': '82bcdd18e664f112149bc29787959516c5c54b5a', 'message': 'Rollup merge of #154894 - WaffleLapkin:mplace_ptr_conversions, r=RalfJung

Slightly refactor mplace<->ptr conversions

split off of https://github.com/rust-lang/rust/pull/154327

r? RalfJung'}, {'sha': '12f51bfdf57502303d43d48a8f18400399bf436f', 'message': "Rollup merge of #154751 - Zalathar:rfail, r=wesleywiser

compiletest: Remove `rfail` support from incremental tests

Incremental revisions beginning with `rfail` would cause the incremental test runner to build the test program, expecting success, and then run the test program, expecting failure.

Expecting incremental tests to fail at runtime is of questionable utility, because in almost all cases an equivalent test program can be made to succeed at runtime instead.

Removing `rfail` support is a small step towards cleaning up compiletest's incremental test runner, and its overall handling of pass/fail expectations.

There was one existing regression test using `rfail` revisions: `tests/incremental/issue-80691-bad-eval-cache.rs`. The test code is complex, and reverting the fix in rust-lang/rust#83220 does not actually cause the test to fail, suggesting that it is no longer a useful regression test. This PR therefore deletes that test.

(An earlier draft of this PR ported the test to run-make, but now it has been removed entirely.)"}, {'sha': 'a2b6e9a2238071e608846db937bcd671feb532a2', 'message': "Rollup merge of #154901 - rustbot:docs-update, r=ehuss

Update books

review: [{'type': 'Design', 'conclusion': '移除 BuiltinLintDiag 枚举，将内置 lint 诊断重构为动态闭包或独立诊断结构。', 'description': '代码变更的主要目的是移除 `BuiltinLintDiag` 枚举，并将之前通过该枚举传递的内置 lint 诊断（如 `ElidedLifetimesInPaths`、`UnusedImports`、`NamedArgumentUsedPositionally`）重构为两种新方式：1) 对于属性相关的 lint，直接使用 `AttributeLintKind`；2) 对于其他内置 lint，使用动态闭包（`DecorateDiagCompat::Dynamic`）或直接在调用点构造诊断结构。这简化了类型系统，但引入了新的复杂性和潜在的性能开销。', 'evidence': '文件 `compiler/rustc_lint_defs/src/lib.rs` 中移除了整个 `BuiltinLintDiag` 枚举的定义。文件 `compiler/rustc_errors/src/decorate_diag.rs` 中 `DecorateDiagCompat` 枚举的 `Builtin` 变体现在包装 `AttributeLintKind` 而非 `BuiltinLintDiag`。文件 `compiler/rustc_builtin_macros/src/format.rs` 中为 `NAMED_ARGUMENTS_USED_POSITIONALLY` lint 创建了一个复杂的动态闭包来构建诊断。', 'advice': '重构方向是合理的，旨在解耦和简化。但需注意动态闭包（`DecorateDiagCompat::Dynamic`）的使用可能带来轻微运行时开销（分配和动态调用）。应确保这些闭包是轻量的，并且所有调用点都正确迁移。对于 `format.rs` 中的闭包，确保其逻辑（特别是 `span_to_snippet` 调用和字符串处理）是高效且正确的。同时，检查是否有其他遗留的对 `BuiltinLintDiag` 的引用需要清理。', 'confidence': 0.9, 'severity': 'Medium'}, {'type': 'Readability', 'conclusion': '`format.rs` 中内联的动态闭包逻辑复杂，降低了可读性。', 'description': '在 `compiler/rustc_builtin_macros/src/format.rs` 中，用于构建 `NamedArgumentUsedPositionally` 诊断的逻辑被内联为一个复杂的闭包。这个闭包包含了条件判断、字符串操作和 `span_to_snippet` 调用，使得代码行数较多且意图不够清晰，不利于维护。', 'evidence': '`compiler/rustc_builtin_macros/src/format.rs` 第 611-644 行：一个多行的 `DecorateDiagCompat::Dynamic(Box::new(move |dcx, level, sess| { ... }))` 闭包。', 'advice': '考虑将这个闭包逻辑提取到一个独立的函数中，即使它只在此处使用。这可以提高可读性，并允许添加更详细的注释来解释逻辑。函数签名可以类似于 `fn build_named_arg_diagnostic(...) -> DecorateDiagCompat`。', 'confidence': 0.8, 'severity': 'Low'}, {'type': 'Rightness', 'conclusion': '`resolve` 模块中 lint 发射逻辑正确迁移至动态闭包。', 'description': '在 `compiler/rustc_resolve/src/check_unused.rs` 和 `compiler/rustc_resolve/src/late.rs` 中，之前使用 `BuiltinLintDiag::UnusedImports` 和 `BuiltinLintDiag::ElidedLifetimesInPaths` 的代码，现在正确地使用了 `dyn_buffer_lint_any` 方法并传递动态闭包来构建相应的诊断结构（`errors::UnusedImports` 和 `errors::ElidedLifetimesInPaths`）。逻辑迁移完整，使用了新定义的诊断结构体。', 'evidence': '`compiler/rustc_resolve/src/check_unused.rs` 第 497-530 行：使用闭包构建 `errors::UnusedImports`。`compiler/rustc_resolve/src/late.rs` 第 2332-2352 行：使用闭包构建 `errors::ElidedLifetimesInPaths`。', 'advice': '迁移正确。确保新引入的 `errors` 模块（在 `rustc_resolve/src/errors.rs` 中）中的诊断结构体定义完整且与之前的行为一致。', 'confidence': 0.95, 'severity': 'Low'}, {'type': 'Design', 'conclusion': '`DecorateDiagCompat` 枚举现在专门处理 review: [{'type': 'Rightness', 'conclusion': '代码主要实现了在解析lint属性时允许编译恢复', 'description': '未发现明显问题。代码变更将`rustc_attr_parsing::ShouldEmit::Nothing`替换为`rustc_attr_parsing::ShouldEmit::ErrorsAndLints { recovery: Recovery::Allowed }`，并添加了回归测试。这允许在解析lint属性（如`#[expect]`）出错时，编译器可以继续运行（恢复）而不是立即停止，从而修复了内部编译器错误（ICE）。', 'evidence': '', 'advice': '', 'confidence': 0.9, 'severity': 'Low'}]

review_comments:
["I'm surprised by the diff on `deriving-all-codegen.stdout`. The code looks like it goes to some effort to only produce `#[allow(unreachable_code)]` on types where it's needed (those involving `!`) and yet the output has that attribute on the derived `clone` for every type.

What I was expecting: all the existing examples in `deriving-all-codegen.stdout` would be unchanged, and there would be some new types added that contain `!` and have `unreachable_code` in their output.

*[View changes since this review](https://triagebot.infra.rust-lang.org/gh-changes-since/rust-lang/rust/154910/fff9e4432233df859e4fadb30ab7f0eda22580a6..2272c288ee0bc11cf2076b76ef9f7c76aff84a58)*", 'Changes to the code generated for builtin derived traits.
